name: ExtensionTransport_Intent
title: R5: unknown | proposal | plan | order | original-order | reflex-order | filler-order | instance-order | option (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Transport.intent` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Transport.intent` 1..1 `code`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `Transport.intent` 1..1 `code`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Transport.intent` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Transport` is not mapped.
Element `Transport.intent` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: unknown | proposal | plan | order | original-order | reflex-order | filler-order | instance-order | option (new)
element.comment:
Element `Transport.intent` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Transport` is not mapped.
Element `Transport.intent` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This element is immutable.  Proposed transports, planned transports, etc. must be distinct instances.

In most cases, Transports will have an intent of "order".

element.short: unknown | proposal | plan | order | original-order | reflex-order | filler-order | instance-order | option
element.definition: Indicates the "level" of actionability associated with the Transport, i.e. i+R[9]Cs this a proposed transport, a planned transport, an actionable transport, etc.
element.comment:
This element is immutable.  Proposed transports, planned transports, etc. must be distinct instances.

In most cases, Transports will have an intent of "order".

binding.description: Distinguishes whether the transport is a proposal, plan or full order.
